title: Neuropeltis integripetala
intro: Neuropeltis integripetala, commonly known as the African snail vine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, Neuropeltis integripetala has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neuropeltis integripetala include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Neuropeltis integripetala is often used in traditional medicine to prepare decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Neuropeltis integripetala is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Neuropeltis, which is a group of plants native to Africa. Neuropeltis integripetala is a climber, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and showy white flowers. The plant is found in tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, where it thrives in humid environments. Neuropeltis integripetala is also known for its ability to absorb nutrients from the air, a process called "heterotrophic nutrition."
